[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|ISO=JP-22}} |shape = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} |construction_period = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial_facility = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |artifacts = Sue ware and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of burial mounds in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular burial mound was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == Dimensions == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue ware fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue ware fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Cultural Heritage Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |artifacts = Sue ware and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of burial mounds in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular burial mound was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == Dimensions == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue ware fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue ware fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Cultural Heritage Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|ISO=JP-22}} |shape = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} |construction_period = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial_facility = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |artifacts = Sue ware and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of burial mounds in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular burial mound was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == Dimensions == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue ware fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue ware fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Cultural Heritage Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|ISO=JP-22}} |shape = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} |construction_period = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial_facility = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |artifacts = Sue ware and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of burial mounds in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular burial mound was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == Dimensions == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue ware fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue ware fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Cultural Heritage Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|ISO=JP-22}} |shape = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} |construction_period = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial_facility = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |artifacts = Sue ware and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of burial mounds in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular burial mound was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == Dimensions == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue ware fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue ware fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Cultural Heritage Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|ISO=JP-22}} |shape = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} |construction_period = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial_facility = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |artifacts = Sue ware and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of burial mounds in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular burial mound was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == Dimensions == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue ware fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue ware fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Cultural Heritage Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|ISO=JP-22}} |shape = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} |construction_period = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial_facility = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |artifacts = Sue ware and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of burial mounds in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular burial mound was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == Dimensions == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue ware fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue ware fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Cultural Heritage Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|ISO=JP-22}} |shape = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} |construction_period = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial_facility = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |artifacts = Sue ware and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of burial mounds in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular burial mound was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == Dimensions == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue ware fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue ware fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Cultural Heritage Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |artifacts = Sue ware and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of burial mounds in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular burial mound was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == Dimensions == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue ware fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue ware fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Cultural Heritage Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|ISO=JP-22}} |shape = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} |construction_period = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial_facility = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |artifacts = Sue ware and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of burial mounds in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular burial mound was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == Dimensions == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue ware fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue ware fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Cultural Heritage Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|ISO=JP-22}} |shape = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} |construction_period = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial_facility = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |artifacts = Sue ware and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of burial mounds in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular burial mound was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == Dimensions == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue ware fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue ware fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Cultural Heritage Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == Dimensions == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue ware fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue ware fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Cultural Heritage Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|ISO=JP-22}} |shape = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} |construction_period = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial_facility = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |artifacts = Sue ware and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of burial mounds in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular burial mound was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == Dimensions == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue ware fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue ware fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Cultural Heritage Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Cultural Heritage Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount
