[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|ISO=JP-22}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} |built = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City other_designation Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|ISO=JP-22}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} |built = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City other_designation Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|ISO=JP-22}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} |built = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City other_designation Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|omitted=JP-22}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} |built = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City other_designation Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|omitted=JP-22}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} |built = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City other_designation Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|omitted=JP-22}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} |built = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. Artifacts primarily consist of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == Artifacts == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City other_designation Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|omitted=JP-22}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} |built = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial = Stone casket |dimensions = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer dimensions: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. burial goods primarily consist of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later artifacts indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City other_designation Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses artifacts from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|omitted=JP-22}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} |built = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial = Stone casket |area = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer area: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. burial goods primarily consist of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City other_designation Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses burial goods from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|omitted=JP-22}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} |built = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial = Stone casket |area = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer area: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. burial goods primarily consist of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City other_designation Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses burial goods from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|omitted=JP-22}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} |built = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial = Stone casket |area = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer area: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |label_position = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. burial goods primarily consist of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Information == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City other_designation Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses burial goods from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_designation Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|omitted=JP-22}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} |built = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial = Stone casket |area = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer area: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |map_dot_label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. burial goods primarily consist of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Intypeation == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City other_other_designation Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses burial goods from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_other_designation Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City other_other_designation Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Intypeation == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|omitted=JP-22}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} |built = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial = Stone casket |area = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer area: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |map_dot_label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. burial goods primarily consist of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Intypeation == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City other_other_designation Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses burial goods from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite
