[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Tomb]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|omitted=JP-22}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} |built = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial = Stone casket |area = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer area: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |map_dot_label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. burial goods primarily consist of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Intypeation == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Historic Site Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses burial goods from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Historic Site Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Historic Site Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Intypeation == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}{{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}} |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|9|12.7998|N|138|51|39.3679|E|omitted=JP-22}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} |built = Early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}} |burial = Stone casket |area = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer area: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |map_dot_label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, {{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. burial goods primarily consist of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, dating the tomb to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed stone coffins from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early {{ill|8th century|ja|8世紀}}, although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to {{ill|Sanage Kiln|ja|猿投窯}}.{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Intypeation == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Historic Site Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses burial goods from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 =　清水柳北1号墳 |画像 = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|清水柳北1号古墳]] |画像キャプション =清水柳北1号墳のレプリカ |所属 = 清水柳北遺跡 |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[沼津市]] |位置 =沼津工業団地 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 9|緯度秒 = 12.7998 |経度度 = 138|経度分 = 51|経度秒 = 39.3679 |ISO = JP-22 |形状 = 上円下方墳 |築造年代 = 8世紀初頭 |埋葬施設 = 石櫃 |規模 = 標高約150m
上円部直径9m
下方部北辺12.4m
周溝外縁部北辺20.3m　西辺20.2m　東辺20.0m　南辺20.2m
周溝上部幅12.4m　底幅約1.0m　深さ約1.0m |被葬者 = |出土品 =須恵器等 |史跡指定 = |特記事項 ={{Sfn|山本|2009|pp=9–12}} |地図 = Japan Shizuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = 上円下方墳 |ラベル =清水柳北
1号墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=清水柳北1号墳の所在地|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''清水柳北1号墳'''（しみずやなぎきたいちごうふん）は、[[静岡県]][[沼津市]]足高尾上にある[[上円下方墳]]である{{Sfn|沼津市史編さん委員会・沼津市教育委員会|2002|p=391}}。 ==概要== 清水柳北1号墳は、第二沼津鉄工団地内で発見され、同団地内で移築されている{{Sfn|平凡社地名資料センター|2000}}。[[1986年]]（[[昭和]]61年）の調査により、下方部の上に上円部が重ねられた上円下方墳であることが判明した。古墳の外周には幅約1メートル、一辺20メートルの方形の周濠が巡らされている。出土遺物は、[[須恵器]]が主であり、古墳の築造時期は、[[8世紀]]前葉と位置付けられている{{Sfn|山本|2009|p=9}}。 ==発掘の経緯== 以前から墳墓群の存在は知られていたが、[[1985年]]（昭和60年）[[沼津工業団地]]の第二工業団地建設計画に伴う詳細分布調査で、尾根の中央部に円墳状の墳墓が確認され、「清水柳北1号墳」と名付けられた。南側に二基の墳丘が確認され2号墳・3号墳と名付けられた。1986年（昭和61年）6月の事前の発掘調査にて上円下方墳であることが判明した。周溝に石櫃があった{{Sfn|沼津市史編さん委員会・沼津市教育委員会|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|1990|p=404}}。 ==規模== 下記の通り{{Sfn|沼津市史編さん委員会・沼津市教育委員会|2002|p=393}}。 *標高　145{{〜}}165メートル *北辺　20.3メートル *西辺　20.2メートル *東辺　20.0メートル *南辺　20.2メートル ==出土品== *石櫃 **周溝東側に当たる凹地に大部分が埋まった形で検出された。3片の[[凝灰岩]]を主体としており、後世の耕作中に散乱したと考えられる断片も周囲から見つかっている。中心部には復元した径35センチメートル程度の丸底の穴がある。だが、石櫃周辺からは蔵骨器に用いたと見られる遺物は見つかっていない。また、[[火葬]]骨も検出されなかった{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|1990|p=414}}。 **石櫃の凝灰岩は[[愛鷹山]]裾部の古墳群のくり抜き式[[石棺]]の石材と同じものであり、沼津市内では江浦凝灰岩と呼ばれる静浦地区のものが知られている{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|1990|p=424}}。 *須恵器 **古墳のある一帯は畑として開墾された土地であった{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|1990|p=404}}。調査時に、墳丘近くの耕作土の斜面から須恵器破片が検出された。発見状況から古墳に関連するものとして推定されている主なものは、蓋坏の蓋、坏身、長頸壷などである。これらは完形品が後世掘り出されて割られて捨てられたものと推定されている{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|1990|p=418}}。これら須恵器はその様相から8世紀前半のものと考えられているが、周囲には少し時代が下ったものもあり、祭祀が継続されていた可能性が発掘調査報告書の中で指摘されている{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|1990|p=687}}。 **須恵器の多くは青灰色であり、[[湖西窯|湖西産]]と思われるが、坏蓋の一部は灰緑色で、[[猿投窯|猿投産]]と推定される{{Sfn|山本|2009|p=12}}。 ==現地情報== '''所在地''' *静岡県沼津市足高尾上 [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|清水柳北1号墳の石櫃（レプリカ）]] '''関連施設''' * [[沼津市文化財センター]]（沼津市志下） - 清水柳北1号墳出土品等を保管。 ==出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=山本|first=惠一|title=清水柳北1号墳について|date=2009-11|year=2009|publisher=ニュ-・サイエンス社|journal=月刊考古学ジャーナル|issue=592|naid=40016904039|pages=9-12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=平凡社地名資料センター|date=2000-10|year=2000|title=日本歴史地名大系第22巻 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=沼津市史編さん委員会・沼津市教育委員会|date=2002-03|year=2002|title=沼津市史 資料編 考古|publisher=沼津市|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=沼津市教育委員会|date=1990-03|year=1990|title=沼津市文化財調査報告書第48集 清水柳北遺跡発掘調査報告書その2|publisher=沼津市教育委員会|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=沼津市教育委員会|date=2009-03|year=2009|title=沼津市文化財調査報告書第96集 清水柳北遺跡（第3次）発掘調査報告書|publisher=沼津市教育委員会|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=富士市文化振興課|title=愛鷹山と生きる|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=静岡県富士市永田町1-100|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=富士山かぐや姫ミュージアム秋のテーマ展　令和3年度沼津市・富士市連携埋蔵文化財活用特別展示事業}} == 外部リンク == *[https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm 沼津市公式ホームページ] {{デフォルトソート:しみすやなききたいちこうふん}} [[Category:静岡県の古墳]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:上円下方墳]]

周溝上部幅12.4m　底幅約1.0m　深さ約1.0m |被葬者 = |出土品 =須恵器等 |史跡指定 = |特記事項 ={{Sfn|山本|2009|pp=9–12}} |地図 = Japan Shizuoka |地図2 = |アイコン = 上円下方墳 |ラベル =清水柳北
1号墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=清水柳北1号墳の所在地|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''清水柳北1号墳'''（しみずやなぎきたいちごうふん）は、[[静岡県]][[沼津市]]足高尾上にある[[上円下方墳]]である{{Sfn|沼津市史編さん委員会・沼津市教育委員会|2002|p=391}}。 ==概要== 清水柳北1号墳は、第二沼津鉄工団地内で発見され、同団地内で移築されている{{Sfn|平凡社地名資料センター|2000}}。[[1986年]]（[[昭和]]61年）の調査により、下方部の上に上円部が重ねられた上円下方墳であることが判明した。古墳の外周には幅約1メートル、一辺20メートルの方形の周濠が巡らされている。出土遺物は、[[須恵器]]が主であり、古墳の築造時期は、[[8世紀]]前葉と位置付けられている{{Sfn|山本|2009|p=9}}。 ==発掘の経緯== 以前から墳墓群の存在は知られていたが、[[1985年]]（昭和60年）[[沼津工業団地]]の第二工業団地建設計画に伴う詳細分布調査で、尾根の中央部に円墳状の墳墓が確認され、「清水柳北1号墳」と名付けられた。南側に二基の墳丘が確認され2号墳・3号墳と名付けられた。1986年（昭和61年）6月の事前の発掘調査にて上円下方墳であることが判明した。周溝に石櫃があった{{Sfn|沼津市史編さん委員会・沼津市教育委員会|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|1990|p=404}}。 ==規模== 下記の通り{{Sfn|沼津市史編さん委員会・沼津市教育委員会|2002|p=393}}。 *標高　145{{〜}}165メートル *北辺　20.3メートル *西辺　20.2メートル *東辺　20.0メートル *南辺　20.2メートル ==出土品== *石櫃 **周溝東側に当たる凹地に大部分が埋まった形で検出された。3片の[[凝灰岩]]を主体としており、後世の耕作中に散乱したと考えられる断片も周囲から見つかっている。中心部には復元した径35センチメートル程度の丸底の穴がある。だが、石櫃周辺からは蔵骨器に用いたと見られる遺物は見つかっていない。また、[[火葬]]骨も検出されなかった{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|1990|p=414}}。 **石櫃の凝灰岩は[[愛鷹山]]裾部の古墳群のくり抜き式[[石棺]]の石材と同じものであり、沼津市内では江浦凝灰岩と呼ばれる静浦地区のものが知られている{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|1990|p=424}}。 *須恵器 **古墳のある一帯は畑として開墾された土地であった{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|1990|p=404}}。調査時に、墳丘近くの耕作土の斜面から須恵器破片が検出された。発見状況から古墳に関連するものとして推定されている主なものは、蓋坏の蓋、坏身、長頸壷などである。これらは完形品が後世掘り出されて割られて捨てられたものと推定されている{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|1990|p=418}}。これら須恵器はその様相から8世紀前半のものと考えられているが、周囲には少し時代が下ったものもあり、祭祀が継続されていた可能性が発掘調査報告書の中で指摘されている{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|1990|p=687}}。 **須恵器の多くは青灰色であり、[[湖西窯|湖西産]]と思われるが、坏蓋の一部は灰緑色で、[[猿投窯|猿投産]]と推定される{{Sfn|山本|2009|p=12}}。 ==現地情報== '''所在地''' *静岡県沼津市足高尾上 [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|清水柳北1号墳の石櫃（レプリカ）]] '''関連施設''' * [[沼津市文化財センター]]（沼津市志下） - 清水柳北1号墳出土品等を保管。 ==出典== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=山本|first=惠一|title=清水柳北1号墳について|date=2009-11|year=2009|publisher=ニュ-・サイエンス社|journal=月刊考古学ジャーナル|issue=592|naid=40016904039|pages=9-12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=平凡社地名資料センター|date=2000-10|year=2000|title=日本歴史地名大系第22巻 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=沼津市史編さん委員会・沼津市教育委員会|date=2002-03|year=2002|title=沼津市史 資料編 考古|publisher=沼津市|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=沼津市教育委員会|date=1990-03|year=1990|title=沼津市文化財調査報告書第48集 清水柳北遺跡発掘調査報告書その2|publisher=沼津市教育委員会|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=沼津市教育委員会|date=2009-03|year=2009|title=沼津市文化財調査報告書第96集 清水柳北遺跡（第3次）発掘調査報告書|publisher=沼津市教育委員会|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=富士市文化振興課|title=愛鷹山と生きる|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=静岡県富士市永田町1-100|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=富士山かぐや姫ミュージアム秋のテーマ展　令和3年度沼津市・富士市連携埋蔵文化財活用特別展示事業}} == 外部リンク == *[https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm 沼津市公式ホームページ] {{デフォルトソート:しみすやなききたいちこうふん}} [[Category:静岡県の古墳]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:上円下方墳]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Shimizu Yanagikita kofun]][[ja:清水柳北1号墳]][[zh:清水柳北1號墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30114518]] Wikidata name: Shimizu Yanagi Kita 1-go Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Numazu|Numazu]] |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{Coord|35.153556|N|138.860936|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |built = Early [[:en:8th century|8th century]] |burial = Stone casket |area = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer area: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |map_dot_label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |architectural_styles = {{ill|round-topped burial mound|qid=Q11358496}} }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, [[:en:Numazu|Numazu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[:en:1986|1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. burial goods primarily consist of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], dating the tomb to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[:en:1986|1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]]. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed Sarcophaguss from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]], although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosei Kiln|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湖西窯跡群}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to [[:en:Sanage Kiln|Sanage Kiln]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Intypeation == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Historic Site Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu City Historic Site Center|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses burial goods from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Historic Site Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Historic Site Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|清水柳北1号墳|version=103216214|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Numazu]] [[Category:Joenkahofun]] }} [[ja:清水柳北1号墳]] [[Category:History of Numazu]] [[Category:Kofun of Shizuoka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shimizu Yanagikita kofun}} [[zh:清水柳北1號墳]] {{wikidata link|Q30114518}}

The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]]. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed Sarcophaguss from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]], although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosei Kiln|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湖西窯跡群}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to [[:en:Sanage Kiln|Sanage Kiln]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Intypeation == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * Upper round diameter: 9 m

Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |map_dot_label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |architectural_styles = {{ill|round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}} }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, [[:en:Numazu|Numazu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[:en:1986|1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. burial goods primarily consist of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], dating the tomb to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[:en:1986|1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]]. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed Sarcophaguss from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]], although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosei Kiln|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湖西窯跡群}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to [[:en:Sanage Kiln|Sanage Kiln]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Intypeation == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Numazu|Numazu]] |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{Coord|35.153556|N|138.860936|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |built = Early [[:en:8th century|8th century]] |burial = Stone casket |area = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer area: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |map_dot_label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |architectural_styles = {{ill|round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}} }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, [[:en:Numazu|Numazu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[:en:1986|1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. burial goods primarily consist of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], dating the tomb to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[:en:1986|1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]]. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed Sarcophaguss from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]], although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosei Kiln|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湖西窯跡群}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to [[:en:Sanage Kiln|Sanage Kiln]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Intypeation == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Historic Site Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu City Historic Site Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses burial goods from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Shimizu Yanagikita kofun]][[ja:清水柳北1号墳]][[zh:清水柳北1號墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q30114518]] Wikidata name: Shimizu Yanagi Kita 1-go Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Numazu|Numazu]] |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{Coord|35.153556|N|138.860936|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |built = Early [[:en:8th century|8th century]] |burial = Stone casket |area = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer area: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |map_dot_label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |architectural_styles = {{ill|round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}} }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, [[:en:Numazu|Numazu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[:en:1986|1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. burial goods primarily consist of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], dating the tomb to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[:en:1986|1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]]. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed Sarcophaguss from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]], although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosei Kiln|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湖西窯跡群}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to [[:en:Sanage Kiln|Sanage Kiln]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Intypeation == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Historic Site Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu City Historic Site Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses burial goods from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Historic Site Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Historic Site Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|清水柳北1号墳|version=103216214|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Numazu]] [[Category:Joenkahofun]] }} [[ja:清水柳北1号墳]] [[Category:History of Numazu]] [[Category:Kofun of Shizuoka prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shimizu Yanagikita kofun}} [[zh:清水柳北1號墳]] {{wikidata link|Q30114518}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |map_dot_label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |architectural_styles = {{ill|round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}} }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, [[:en:Numazu|Numazu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[:en:1986|1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. burial goods primarily consist of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], dating the tomb to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[:en:1986|1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]]. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed Sarcophaguss from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]], although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosei Kiln|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湖西窯跡群}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to [[:en:Sanage Kiln|Sanage Kiln]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Intypeation == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Historic Site Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu City Historic Site Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses burial goods from [[sn:commons:Category:Shimizu Yanagikita kofun]][[ja:清水柳北1号墳]][[zh:清水柳北1號墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q30114518]] Wikidata name: Shimizu Yanagi Kita 1-go Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Numazu|Numazu]] |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{Coord|35.153556|N|138.860936|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |built = Early [[:en:8th century|8th century]] |burial = Stone casket |area = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer area: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |map_dot_label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |architectural_styles = {{ill|round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}} }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, [[:en:Numazu|Numazu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[:en:1986|1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. burial goods primarily consist of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], dating the tomb to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[:en:1986|1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]]. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed Sarcophaguss from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]], although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosei Kiln|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湖西窯跡群}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to [[:en:Sanage Kiln|Sanage Kiln]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Intypeation == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Historic Site Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu City Historic Site Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses burial goods from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Historic Site Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Historic Site Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |map_dot_label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |architectural_styles = {{ill|round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}} }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, [[:en:Numazu|Numazu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[:en:1986|1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. burial goods primarily consist of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], dating the tomb to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[:en:1986|1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]]. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed Sarcophaguss from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]], although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosei Kiln|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湖西窯跡群}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to [[:en:Sanage Kiln|Sanage Kiln]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Intypeation == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Historic Site Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu City Historic Site Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses burial goods from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Volume 22: Shizuoka|publisher=Heibonsha|year=2000|isbn=9784582490220|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|title=Numazu City History, Archaeological Materials Volume|publisher=Numazu City|year=2002|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Historic Site Investigation Report No. 48: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Historic Site Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi Cultural Promotion Division|title=Living with Mount Ashitaka|origdate=2021-09-18|date=2021-09-18|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/102009|location=Fujishi, Shizuoka|doi=10.24484/sitereports.102009|series=Special Exhibition Collaboration Project between Numazu and Fujishi, 2021}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/shimizu.htm Official Numazu City Website] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|清水柳北1号墳|version=103216214|comment=Imported full ja This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |image = [[File:Shimizu Yanagikita Kofun Number 1.jpg|300px|Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] |image_caption = Replica of Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Numazu|Numazu]] |area = Numazu Industrial Complex |coordinates = {{Coord|35.153556|N|138.860936|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |built = Early [[:en:8th century|8th century]] |burial = Stone casket |area = Elevation: approx. 150 m
Upper round diameter: 9 m
Lower square northern side: 12.4 m
Moat outer area: Northern side 20.3 m, Western side 20.2 m, Eastern side 20.0 m, Southern side 20.2 m
Moat top width: 12.4 m; bottom width: approx. 1.0 m; depth: approx. 1.0 m |burial goods = Sue pottery and other items |map = Japan Shizuoka |map_dot_label = Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom |architectural_styles = {{ill|round-topped burial mound|WD=Q11358496}} }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=right|frame-width=300|text=Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun location|coord={{coord2|35|9|12.79|N|138|51|39.36|E}}}} '''Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun''' (清水柳北1号墳) is a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in Ashitaka-Ogami, [[:en:Numazu|Numazu]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}} == Overview == Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was discovered within the Second Numazu Industrial Complex and later relocated within the same area.{{Sfn|Heibonsha Place name Data Center|2000}} An investigation in [[:en:1986|1986]] (Shōwa 61) confirmed its structure as a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a lower rectangular section overlain by an upper circular section. Surrounding the tomb is a rectangular moat about 1 meter wide and 20 meters along each side. burial goods primarily consist of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], dating the tomb to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=9}} == Excavation History == The presence of Kofuns in the area had been known for some time. During a detailed survey in [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), associated with the development of the Second Numazu Industrial Complex, a circular Kofun was identified at the center of the ridge and named "Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun." Two additional mounds were identified to the south and named No. 2 and No. 3 Kofun. A pre-construction excavation in [[:en:1986|1986]] (Shōwa 61) revealed that Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun was a {{ill|Jōen-kahō-fun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōen-kahō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. A stone casket was discovered in the moat.{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=391}}{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=404}} == area == * Elevation: 145–165 meters * Northern side: 20.3 meters * Western side: 20.2 meters * Eastern side: 20.0 meters * Southern side: 20.2 meters{{Sfn|Numazu City History Compilation Committee & Education Board|2002|p=393}} == burial goods == === Stone Casket === * A stone casket was found buried in a hollow on the eastern side of the moat. The casket was primarily composed of three pieces of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]]. Fragments scattered by later agricultural activities were also discovered. The casket features a central circular depression about 35 cm in diameter, but no burial-related items such as urns or cremated remains were found nearby.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=414}} * The tuff matches the material used in hollowed Sarcophaguss from tomb groups near the base of Mount Ashitaka. Locally, the tuff is referred to as "Numazu tuff," originating from the Shizuura area.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|p=424}} === Sue Ware === * Sue pottery fragments, including lids, bowls, and long-necked jars, were recovered from the soil near the mound, suggesting their association with the tomb. These items are thought to date to the early [[:en:8th century|8th century]], although some later burial goods indicate continued ritual use.{{Sfn|Numazu City Education Board|1990|pp=418,687}} * Most Sue pottery fragments are bluish-gray, likely originating from {{ill|Kosei Kiln|ja|湖西窯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosei Kiln|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湖西窯跡群}}. However, some greenish-gray pieces, such as bowl lids, are attributed to [[:en:Sanage Kiln|Sanage Kiln]].{{Sfn|Yamamoto|2009|p=12}} == Current Intypeation == '''location''' * Numazu Industrial Complex, Ashitaka-Ogami, Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture [[File:Shimizuyanagi Kita1 Sekihitsu.jpg|thumb|Replica of the stone casket from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun]] '''Related Facility''' * {{ill|Numazu City Historic Site Center|ja|沼津市文化財センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu City Historic Site Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Numazu City, Shige) houses burial goods from Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Yamamoto|first=Keiichi|title=About Shimizu Yanagikita No. 1 Kofun|date=2009-11|publisher=New Science Publishing|journal=Monthly Archaeology Journal|issue=592|pages=9–12|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Heibonsha Place name Data Center|title=Japanese Historical Place name Encyclopedia, Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Part 2)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=1990|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Numazu City Education Board|title=Numazu City Historic Site Investigation Report No. 96: Shimizu Yanagikita Archaeological Investigation Report (Third Phase)|publisher=Numazu City Education Board|year=2009|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=Fujishi
